[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Once I reached the dugout, one of the first people I saw was Albert Pujols. 
 The first thing he said was, âLet me call your wife.â Then he grabbed a phone from Tim Mead, our p.r. guy, and started dialing Danielle. It was such a thoughtful thing for Albert to do. My wife and I were so appreciative that he connected the two of us right away. 
 When she got the call from Albert, my wife was watching the game on TV back in Anaheim. So she had seen what just happened to me. She knew I was doing O.K. since she had watchedÂ me walk off the field, and she was probably expecting to hear from somebody about my condition.  3. MLB cracks down on sticky stuff (June 22) 
 There was a time, before MLBâs decision to systematically monitor the illegal use of grip-enhancing substances by players throughout the game, when the average fan gave absolutely zero thought to the use of whatâs now commonly known as âsticky stuff.â By seasonâs end, the mid-inning checks of pitchers by umpires were routine. But in between, following a Sports Illustrated investigation by Stephanie Apstein and Alex Prewitt, the leagueâs abrupt decision to crack down on what was described by one MLB team executive as âthe biggest scandal in sportsâ sent players into a frenzy.

[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: In the fifth, Pujols hit a grounder to Shaw, who threw high to Dustin Pedroia at second looking for the double play. Trout slid hard into second and was safe while Pedroia quickly shot a bullet to first. Pujols was called safe, but Farrell challenged. After an official replay review, the call on the field stood. 
 Angels:Matt Shoemaker hopes to keep his dominant run going when he takes the ball for Sunday's rubber game at Fenway Park, with first pitch set for 10:35 a.m. PT. Shoemaker has a 1.87 ERA over his last eight starts, striking out 68 and walking only five in 57 2/3 innings. But he somehow has only one win in that stretch.
